name: Saffron Kulfi Pops
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 6-8 quart pan over high heat, stir milk and sugar until simmering.
2. Over medium-high heat, boil the mixture until reduced to 2 cups, about 25-35 minutes, stirring often. If the milk threatens to boil over, slide the pan partially off the heat.
3. Let cool; to speed cooling, set the pan in ice water.
4. Place saffron in a small bowl. Add boiling water and stir, letting stand for 5 minutes. Break up threads with a spoon.
5. Scrape the mixture into the warm reduced milk mixture.
6. Set paper cups in a rimmed pan. Or, to make cones, cut 8 pieces of parchment or waxed paper into 7-1/2 inch squares. Fold each piece in half to make a triangle.
7. With the long edge toward you, bring one of the 45-degree angles to the top of the triangle, then roll toward the other angle. To close the hole at the bottom, starting from the top, press one inside sheet to the opposite side. Tape the cone in a few places to hold it together.
8. Support each cone, pointed end down, in a cup slightly taller than the cone; set cups in a rimmed pan.
9. Divide the milk mixture among the cups or cones. Freeze until kulfi is thick but not hard, about 1 to 1-1/2 hours; then, if desired, push an ice cream stick into each container.
10. Freeze until firm, about 2 hours longer. To eat, peel off paper. To store, seal the kulfi (still in cups or cones) in a large plastic bag; freeze for up to 2 weeks.
Per serving: 143 calories; 6 grams protein; 6.1 grams fat; (3.8 grams saturated fat); 17 grams carbohydrates; 90 milligrams sodium; 26 milligrams cholesterol.
